perturbation.original_text: Each Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party shall not be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 4 explicitly states that each party is responsible for breaches of the agreement by its representatives. By changing 'shall be responsible' to 'shall not be responsible', the agreement now contradicts itself on a fundamental point of liability. This change removes any liability of the parties for their Representatives actions, creating a conflict with the purpose of the document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party understands and agrees that money damages may not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by either Party or any of its Representatives and that the Party against which such breach is committed shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach or threat thereof. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach by either Party of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity to the Party against which such breach is committed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that each party 'shall not be responsible' for any breach of the Agreement by its Representatives, which directly contradicts the contradicted text stating that 'money damages may not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by either Party or any of its Representatives'. This creates a strong contradiction regarding the liability of a party for its representatives actions and makes the document ambiguous, which fits the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: If either Party decides that it does not wish to proceed with a Possible Transaction, it will promptly inform the other Party of that decision. In that case, or at any time upon the request of a Provider for any reason, a Recipient will, promptly after receipt of such notice or request, destroy all Evaluation Material in any way relating to the Provider or its products, services, employees or other assets or liabilities, and no copy or extract thereof (including electronic copies) shall be retained. Notwithstanding the foregoing, (i) a Recipient shall not be required to delete, erase or destroy any Evaluation Material contained in an archived computer backup system stored as a result of automated back-up procedures, and (ii) a Recipient may retain one copy of the Evaluation Material in its Office of the General Counsel solely for record-keeping purposes. The Recipient shall provide to the Provider a certificate of compliance with this provision. Notwithstanding the destruction or retention of the Evaluation Material, the Recipient and its Representatives will continue to be bound by such Recipient’s obligations hereunder with respect to such Evaluation Material.
perturbation.changed_text: If either Party decides that it does not wish to proceed with a Possible Transaction, it may or may not inform the other Party of that decision at their discretion. In that case, or at any time upon the request of a Provider for any reason, a Recipient may, at its sole discretion, retain or destroy all Evaluation Material in any way relating to the Provider or its products, services, employees or other assets or liabilities, and any number of copies or extracts thereof (including electronic copies) may or may not be retained. Notwithstanding the foregoing, (i) a Recipient shall be required to delete, erase or destroy all Evaluation Material contained in an archived computer backup system stored as a result of automated back-up procedures, and (ii) a Recipient must never retain any copy of the Evaluation Material in its Office of the General Counsel solely for record-keeping purposes. The Recipient is not required to provide to the Provider a certificate of compliance with this provision. Notwithstanding the destruction or retention of the Evaluation Material, the Recipient and its Representatives will not continue to be bound by such Recipient’s obligations hereunder with respect to such Evaluation Material.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section 6 mandates prompt notification of transaction termination and destruction of Evaluation Material, with limited exceptions for backups and legal records. It also states that obligations continue even after destruction. The modified text introduces several contradictions. First, it changes 'will promptly inform' to 'may or may not inform,' making notification discretionary. Second, the destruction of Evaluation Material is also made discretionary. Furthermore, the exceptions for backups and legal records are reversed – backups must now be deleted and legal records cannot be retained. Finally, the obligation to be bound is eliminated after destruction or retention. This creates significant internal contradictions within Section 6 itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If either Party decides that it does not wish to proceed with a Possible Transaction, it may or may not inform the other Party of that decision at their discretion. In that case, or at any time upon the request of a Provider for any reason, a Recipient may, at its sole discretion, retain or destroy all Evaluation Material in any way relating to the Provider or its products, services, employees or other assets or liabilities, and any number of copies or extracts thereof (including electronic copies) may or may not be retained. Notwithstanding the foregoing, (i) a Recipient shall be required to delete, erase or destroy all Evaluation Material contained in an archived computer backup system stored as a result of automated back-up procedures, and (ii) a Recipient must never retain any copy of the Evaluation Material in its Office of the General Counsel solely for record-keeping purposes. The Recipient is not required to provide to the Provider a certificate of compliance with this provision. Notwithstanding the destruction or retention of the Evaluation Material, the Recipient and its Representatives will not continue to be bound by such Recipient’s obligations hereunder with respect to such Evaluation Material.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces multiple in-text contradictions within Section 6 itself, as well as against the 'Contradicted Text', which in this case is an exact duplicate. The original text mandates notification, destruction of evaluation material, and continued obligation. The changed text makes all of these discretionary ('may or may not inform', 'may retain or destroy'), reverses exceptions (requiring deletion of backups and prohibiting legal record retention), and eliminates continued obligation, creating direct and irreconcilable conflicts within the document and with the 'Contradicted Text'.

perturbation.original_text: Each Recipient agrees that, for eighteen months from the date of this Agreement, no person in its organization who receives or has access to the Evaluation Material or has knowledge about the Possible Transaction will, directly or indirectly, solicit, or cause the soliciting of, or assist or encourage others in the soliciting of, the employment or consulting services of any of the officers of a Provider or any of its subsidiaries or employees engaged in research and development for Provider or any of its subsidiaries or any other employee of the Provider or any if its subsidiaries with whom the Recipient has had contact in connection with its evaluation of a Possible Transaction or of whom it has become aware as a result of its receipt of any Evaluation Material, so long as they are employed by the Provider or any of its subsidiaries and for two months thereafter (each, a “ Covered Employee ”). A Recipient is not prohibited from: (i) soliciting by means of a general advertisement not directed at any particular individual or the employees of the Provider or its subsidiaries generally, or (ii) engaging any recruiting firm or similar organization to identify or solicit individuals for employment on behalf of such Recipient (and soliciting any person identified by any such recruiting firm or organization) so long as such Recipient does not identify the individuals to be solicited by such recruiting firm or organization. The provisions of this Section 7 shall terminate and be of no further effect with respect to the Covered Employees upon the acquisition of such Provider by a third party.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Recipient agrees that, notwithstanding anything contained in this Agreement, any person in its organization who receives or has access to the Evaluation Material or has knowledge about the Possible Transaction will, directly or indirectly, solicit, or cause the soliciting of, or assist or encourage others in the soliciting of, the employment or consulting services of any of the officers of a Provider or any of its subsidiaries or employees engaged in research and development for Provider or any of its subsidiaries or any other employee of the Provider or any if its subsidiaries with whom the Recipient has had contact in connection with its evaluation of a Possible Transaction or of whom it has become aware as a result of its receipt of any Evaluation Material, so long as they are employed by the Provider or any of its subsidiaries and for two months thereafter (each, a “ Covered Employee ”). A Recipient is required from: (i) soliciting by means of a general advertisement directed at any particular individual or the employees of the Provider or its subsidiaries generally, or (ii) preventing engaging any recruiting firm or similar organization to identify or solicit individuals for employment on behalf of such Recipient (and preventing soliciting any person identified by any such recruiting firm or organization) so long as such Recipient does identify the individuals to be solicited by such recruiting firm or organization. The provisions of this Section 7 shall never terminate and be of further effect with respect to the Covered Employees upon the acquisition of such Provider by a third party.
perturbation.explanation: Section 7 originally restricts the solicitation of the Provider's employees by the Recipient for 18 months, with certain exceptions. The modified text negates the no solicitation part and changes the exceptions. Now any person in the Recipients organization will sollicit the Providers employees, and also now, is required from doing the opposite of the exceptions (soliciting and engaging). The provisions now never terminate. The contradictions will make the rule difficult to enforce.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Recipient agrees that, notwithstanding anything contained in this Agreement, any person in its organization who receives or has access to the Evaluation Material or has knowledge about the Possible Transaction will, directly or indirectly, solicit, or cause the soliciting of, or assist or encourage others in the soliciting of, the employment or consulting services of any of the officers of a Provider or any of its subsidiaries or employees engaged in research and development for Provider or any of its subsidiaries or any other employee of the Provider or any if its subsidiaries with whom the Recipient has had contact in connection with its evaluation of a Possible Transaction or of whom it has become aware as a result of its receipt of any Evaluation Material, so long as they are employed by the Provider or any of its subsidiaries and for two months thereafter (each, a “ Covered Employee ”). A Recipient is required from: (i) soliciting by means of a general advertisement directed at any particular individual or the employees of the Provider or its subsidiaries generally, or (ii) preventing engaging any recruiting firm or similar organization to identify or solicit individuals for employment on behalf of such Recipient (and preventing soliciting any person identified by any such recruiting firm or organization) so long as such Recipient does identify the individuals to be solicited by such recruiting firm or organization. The provisions of this Section 7 shall never terminate and be of further effect with respect to the Covered Employees upon the acquisition of such Provider by a third party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text restricted solicitation of the Provider's employees, while the changed text implies that solicitation will happen from any person, which contradicts the original intention of limiting or preventing such actions. Also, the original text had certain exceptions regarding solicitation through general advertisements, but the changed text negates the exceptions by requiring solicitations that were previously prohibited. Furthermore, the termination clause has been changed so that the provisions shall never terminate.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party understands and agrees that money damages may not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by either Party or any of its Representatives and that the Party against which such breach is committed shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach or threat thereof. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach by either Party of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity to the Party against which such breach is committed.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party understands and agrees that money damages may be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by either Party or any of its Representatives and that the Party against which such breach is committed shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach or threat thereof. Such remedies shall be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach by either Party of this Agreement, and there are no other remedies available at law or equity to the Party against which such breach is committed.
perturbation.explanation: Section 12 initially states that money damages may not be a sufficient remedy and that equitable relief can be sought, but such remedies are not exclusive. The modified text states that money damages *may* be sufficient and that the defined remedies *are* the exclusive remedies. This directly contradicts the intent to preserve other legal avenues for resolving disputes. This creates a contradiction because Section 12 first suggests money can't be used as a remedy, then says it can, while also saying, at the same time, there can be no remedy at all
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party understands and agrees that money damages may be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by either Party or any of its Representatives and that the Party against which such breach is committed shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach or threat thereof. Such remedies shall be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach by either Party of this Agreement, and there are no other remedies available at law or equity to the Party against which such breach is committed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself and the original intent of the section. It first states that money damages *may* be sufficient, then says the defined remedies are *exclusive*, effectively negating other legal options, including monetary relief. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, emphasizing the internal contradiction created by the modification. The original text indicated equitable relief was *in addition to* other remedies; the changed text now limits the party to only equitable relief and nothing else, which contradicts the assertion that money damages may be sufficient. This is a clear case of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.
